name: PEM Fuel Cell Operation
description: A proton-exchange-membrane fuel cell oxidizes hydrogen at the anode, conducts protons through the membrane while electrons do work in the external circuit, and reduces oxygen at the cathode; the sluggish oxygen reduction is accelerated by a Pt catalyst.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["H2 fuel + O2 oxidant"]
    N2["Anode HOR: H2 -> 2H+ + 2e−"]
    N3["Protons cross membrane"]
    N4["Electrons via external circuit"]
    N5["Electric work delivered"]
    N6["Cathode ORR: O2 -> H2O"]
    N7{"ORR limits performance?"}
    N8["Pt catalyst lowers barrier"]
    N9["Water + heat removed"]
    N10["Continuous power output"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N2 --> N4
    N3 --> N6
    N4 --> N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 --> N7
    N7 -->|yes| N8
    N8 --> N9
    N7 -->|no| N9
    N9 --> N10

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
